examples: However such "contracts" are imperfect as the impracticality to include every action of the agent whose decisions has an impact on their and the principal's benefits.
The resilience of the two main communities obliges each to recognise the impracticality of its maximal demands.
Mr Bush has probably by now accepted the impracticality of instructing Mr Abbas to go after the militant factions with force.
All these proposals suffer from the problem of extreme impracticality, though.
